office_does: The county clerk maintains Superior Court records, processes filings, and supports court administration and public access.
race_blurb: Incumbent Marty Young faces three challengers with clerk-office, court-reporting, and court-facilitator experience.

candidates.summary: Beck is a veteran court reporter and business owner emphasizing equitable service, efficient operations, quality control, and budget stewardship.
highlights: Has 35 years of court-reporting experience and 31 years as a firm owner [S1].
Emphasizes efficient operations, quality control, and budget stewardship [S1].

spending.basis: Her stated priorities include budget stewardship and operational quality control.

experience.basis: Her extensive court-system and management experience is directly relevant, though not a documented clerk-office record.

candidates.summary: Young is the incumbent clerk with an established management record emphasizing continuity, backlog reduction, compliance, and service.
highlights: Serves as incumbent clerk with an established office-management record [S1].
Emphasizes continuity, backlog reduction, compliance, and service [S1].

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with an established record managing the clerk's office.

candidates.summary: Thompson is a clerk's-office records administrator with more than a decade of service emphasizing procedures, retention, leadership, and records.
highlights: Has more than a decade of clerk-office service [S1].
Emphasizes procedures, retention, leadership, records, and service [S1].

experience.basis: Her decade-plus clerk-office record is directly relevant, though she is not the incumbent clerk.

candidates.summary: Arechiga is a family-law court facilitator with front-line court-user experience emphasizing access, service, processing, and practical administration.
highlights: Works as a family-law court facilitator with front-line court-user experience [S1].
Emphasizes court access, processing, expertise, and practical administration [S1].

experience.basis: Her court-facilitator role supplies directly relevant court-system experience but no documented clerk-office tenure.
